tasks_completed.description: Move context-strip block before Layer 1.F and add false-positive guard in _bulk_decision
tasks_completed.changes: Moved context-strip block (COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED initialization) from line ~1091 to before Layer 1.F (~line 662). Entry gate kept on raw $COMMAND (not COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED — BA spec approach rejected because context stripper removes python3 script paths from compound segments, causing compound forms to evade the entry gate). Added false-positive guard in _bulk_decision Python heredoc: after all DENY checks but before BARE_WRITER check, detects if protected name appears only as a substring of a longer argument token (not standalone path). Emits PURE_READ to allow such commands.
tasks_completed.rationale: BA spec approach failed AC4: context stripper's _process_script_interp removes the script path token from python3 segments, so 'echo test && python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py' loses the protected name from COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED — entry gate would not fire and compound form passes through. Correct fix is to keep entry gate on raw $COMMAND and resolve false positives inside _bulk_decision using token-shape analysis.

tasks_completed.changes: Added TestBulkSentinelFalsePositive class with 4 test methods: test_ac1_graphify_requirement_arg_not_blocked, test_ac2_codex_prompt_arg_not_blocked, test_ac3_bare_invocation_still_allowed, test_ac4_compound_invocation_still_blocked.
tasks_completed.rationale: Regression tests required by AC5. Tests drive real hook process via piped JSON and assert on exit code.

AC1.command: python3 scripts/graphify-query.py --requirement "Add positive regression test for CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID fallback path in write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py"

AC2.command: codex exec --prompt "Please add a test for write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py fallback path"

AC4.evidence: test_ac4_compound_invocation_still_blocked PASSED; compound separator && detected by shlex tokenizer before false-positive guard is reached

AC5.evidence: pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py -v: 12 passed, 0 failed. TestBulkSentinelFalsePositive class with 4 test methods present.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — design gap between two features added at different times (tasks 20260524-205206 + 20260529-092512)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Layer 1.F entry gate was added for fast-fail sentinel protection. Context-strip block was added later (dev-20260529-092512) for other false-positive classes. The two were never integrated. Entry gate uses raw $COMMAND substring match which includes verbatim quoted argument text.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: False-positive guard in _bulk_decision uses shlex-tokenized view of the command. After all compound/recursive-shell/write-action DENY checks pass, detects if the protected name appears only as a substring of a longer argument token (unrelated text) vs. a standalone path token (real execution reference). Arg-text-only case emits PURE_READ (allow). Standalone path case falls through to BARE_WRITER / DENY as before.

deviation_from_ba_spec.ba_spec_approach: Move context-strip block before Layer 1.F; change entry gate grep calls from $COMMAND to $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED
deviation_from_ba_spec.deviation_reason: BA spec approach fails AC4. Context stripper's _process_script_interp removes the script path token from python3 invocation segments. For 'echo test && python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py --output-dir /tmp', the stripper produces 'echo test&&python3   \n' — no protected name. Entry gate using $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED would not fire. Compound form would pass through unchecked.
deviation_from_ba_spec.actual_approach: Keep entry gate on raw $COMMAND (catches all mentions). Fix false positives inside _bulk_decision with token-shape analysis: after all DENY checks pass, if protected name appears only as a substring of a longer argument token (not a standalone path), emit PURE_READ. Compound commands are still denied by the compound-separator DENY check BEFORE the false-positive guard is reached.
deviation_from_ba_spec.context_strip_block_relocation: Still moved per BA spec — COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED is now initialized before Layer 1.F. However, the entry gate does not use it (comment explains why). The variable is available for future use and the relocation is otherwise correct.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: 98 insertions vs 45 minimum: (a) context-strip block relocated adds ~14 lines at new location, removes ~19 at old location (net +14 due to replacement comment); (b) false-positive guard Python logic ~30 lines inside heredoc; (c) test class ~43 lines. The guard logic requires careful token-shape iteration with explicit break semantics — cannot be compressed further without sacrificing clarity for a security-critical hook.

imported_by: all PreToolUse Bash hook invocations system-wide
blast_radius.impact: high — every Bash tool call passes through this hook

dev.qa_notes: Run: python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py -v. Expect 12/12 pass. Key regression to verify: AC4 compound form (echo test && python3 scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py) must still exit 2. The BA spec approach was rejected — see deviation_from_ba_spec for rationale. QA should be aware that the context-strip block was moved earlier (before Layer 1.F) but the entry gate still uses raw $COMMAND.

self_verification.notes: All 12 pytest tests pass including 4 new TestBulkSentinelFalsePositive tests. Build: bash script — syntax check via bash -n not run but hook runs without syntax errors (evidenced by test execution). AC1-AC5 all verified via pytest.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex invocation blocked by the hook under analysis — the prompt PROMPT variable assignment containing the protected script name triggered Layer 1.F entry gate (leading variable assignment with sentinel name in value → DENY by _bulk_decision). This is the same class of false positive as the BA-reported case but for shell variable assignment. Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review substituted. 5 adversarial questions answered: (1) _sentinel_in_arg_only iteration order is safe — standalone-path cases break immediately regardless of order; (2) bypass via echo write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py correctly DENYed (echo not in PURE_READ_VERBS, standalone path token detected); (3) context-strip relocation causes no initialization-order issues; (4) /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel- path in unrelated arg text handled correctly; (5) simpler approaches (adding codex to PURE_READ_VERBS) are fragile — token-shape analysis is the correct general solution.

recommendations: Consider adding a test for the shell variable assignment false positive form (PROMPT='...sentinel...' some-command) — this is still blocked by the leading-variable-assignment DENY in _bulk_decision. This is likely CORRECT behavior (leading var assignment is a suspicious command shape) but could be documented.

The context-strip block relocation (COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED available before Layer 1.F) enables a future optimization: the entry gate could use $COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED for non-script-interpreter, non-compound commands, falling back to $COMMAND otherwise. This is not needed now but the relocation makes it possible.
